title: galium boreale var. hyssopifolium
intro: Galium boreale var. hyssopifolium, also known as Bearberry or Kinnikinnick, is a deciduous shrub native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and its antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in arbutin, a compound that has been shown to have antioxidant and antidiabetic effects, providing health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of urinary tract infections. Galium boreale var. hyssopifolium can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine.
